name: qradar-offenses

outputs.description: Offense followup QRadar.Offense.ID The ID of the offense
outputs.name: QRadar.Offense.Followup

outputs.description: The description of the offense QRadar.Offense.SourceAddress The source addresses that are associated with the offense
outputs.name: QRadar.Offense.Description

outputs.description: The local destination addresses that are associated with the offense. If your offense has a remote destination, you will need to use QRadarFullSearch playbook with the following query:
outputs.name: QRadar.Offense.DestinationAddress

outputs.description: QRadar.Offense.RemoteDestinationCount The remote destination that are associated with the offense. If this value is greater than 0 that means your offense has a remote destination, you will need to use QRadarFullSearch playbook with the following query: SELECT destinationip FROM events WHERE inOffense() GROUP BY destinationip
outputs.name: SELECT destinationip FROM events WHERE inOffense() GROUP BY destinationip

outputs.description: date The time (ISO) when the offense was started. QRadar.Offense.EventCount
outputs.name: QRadar.Offense.StartTime

outputs.description: The number of events that are associated with the offense QRadar.Offense.Magnitude The magnitude of the offense
outputs.name: number

outputs.description: date The time (ISO) when the offense was last updated QRadar.Offense.OffenseType
outputs.name: QRadar.Offense.LastUpdatedTime

outputs.description: The offense type (due to API limitations if username and password were not provided, this value will be the id of offense type) Command Examples !qradar-offenses range=0-1 filter="follow_up = false" headers=ID,Magnitude
outputs.name: string

outputs.description:
"Followup": false, "Description": "Multiple Login Failures for the Same User
 preceded by shachar_test
 containing Failed Login Attempt
", "EventCount": 3,
outputs.name: "Offense": {
